texto: Scientific theories never dictate human values, but they can often cast new light on ethical issues. From a sexual selection viewpoint, moral philosophy and political theory have mostly been attempts to shift male human sexual competitiveness from physical violence to the peaceful accumulation of wealth and status. The rights to life, liberty, and property are cultural inventions that function, in part, to keep males from killing and stealing from one another while they compete to attract sexual partners.
explicacao: Citação de Geoffrey Miller sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Scientific theories never dictate human values, but they can often cast new light on ethical issu…” articula reflexao, ethics, evolution em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como evolution e politics.
